content:
# Ruth Chapter 1 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 880-881 | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/RUT_001_page_001.jpg, RUT_001_page_002.jpg

---

**1:1** And-it-was in-[the]-days of-[the]-judging of-the-judges, and-there-was a-famine in-the-land. And-went a-man from-Beth-lehem of-Judah to-sojourn in-[the]-fields of-Moab, he and-his-wife and-his-two sons.

**1:2** And-[the]-name of-the-man [was] Elimelech, and-[the]-name of-his-wife [was] Naomi, and-[the]-name of-his-two sons [were] Mahlon and-Chilion, Ephrathites from-Beth-lehem of-Judah. And-they-came [to the]-fields of-Moab, and-they-were there.

**1:3** And-died Elimelech, [the]-husband of-Naomi, and-she-was-left, she and-her-two sons.

**1:4** And-they-took for-themselves wives, Moabite-women; [the]-name of-the-one [was] Orpah, and-[the]-name of-the-second [was] Ruth. And-they-dwelt there about ten years.

**1:5** And-died also [the]-two-of-them, Mahlon and-Chilion, and-the-woman was-left from-her-two children and-from-her-husband.

**1:6** And-she-arose, she and-her-daughters-in-law, and-she-returned from-[the]-fields of-Moab, for she-heard in-[the]-field of-Moab that visited YHWH his-people to-give to-them bread.

**1:7** And-she-went-out from the-place which she-was there, and-her-two daughters-in-law with-her, and-they-went on-the-way to-return to the-land of-Judah.

**1:8** And-said Naomi to-her-two daughters-in-law, "Go, return, each-woman to-[the]-house of-her-mother. May-YHWH-do with-you lovingkindness [hesed], as you-have-done with the-dead-ones and-with-me.

**1:9** May-YHWH-grant to-you, and-find rest, each-woman [in the]-house of-her-husband." And-she-kissed them, and-they-lifted-up their-voice and-wept.

**1:10** And-they-said to-her, "No, for with-you we-will-return to-your-people."

**1:11** And-said Naomi, "Return, my-daughters. Why will-you-go with-me? Are-there yet to-me sons in-my-inward-parts, and-they-will-be for-you for-husbands?

**1:12** Return, my-daughters, go, for I-am-too-old from-being for-a-man. If I-said, 'There-is for-me hope,' also if I-were to-a-man the-night, and-also I-bore sons,

**1:13** would-you-therefore wait until which they-grew-up? Would-you-therefore shut-yourselves-up to-not be for-a-man? No, my-daughters, for it-is-bitter to-me exceedingly more-than-you, for [the]-hand of-YHWH has-gone-out against-me."

**1:14** And-they-lifted-up their-voice and-wept again. And-kissed Orpah to-her-mother-in-law, but-Ruth clung [davaq] to-her.

**1:15** And-she-said, "Behold, returned your-sister-in-law to her-people and-to her-gods. Return after your-sister-in-law."

**1:16** And-said Ruth, "Do-not-urge me to-leave-you, to-return from-after-you, for to-where you-go I-will-go, and-where you-lodge I-will-lodge; your-people [are] my-people, and-your-God [is] my-God.

**1:17** Where you-die I-will-die, and-there I-will-be-buried. Thus may-YHWH-do to-me and-thus may-He-add, for [only] death will-separate between-me and-between-you."

**1:18** And-she-saw that she-[was]-strengthening-herself to-go with-her, and-she-ceased to-speak to-her.

**1:19** And-the-two-of-them went until they-came [to] Beth-lehem. And-it-was when-they-came [to] Beth-lehem, and-was-stirred all the-city on-account-of-them, and-they-said, "Is-this Naomi?"

**1:20** And-she-said to-them, "Do-not-call to-me Naomi; call to-me Mara, for the-Almighty has-made-bitter to-me exceedingly.

**1:21** I [was] full [when] I-went, and-empty YHWH has-brought-me-back. Why do-you-call to-me Naomi, and-YHWH has-testified against-me, and-the-Almighty has-done-evil to-me?"

**1:22** And-returned Naomi, and-Ruth the-Moabitess, her-daughter-in-law, with-her, the-[one]-returning from-[the]-fields of-Moab. And-they came [to] Beth-lehem at-[the]-beginning of-[the]-harvest of-barley.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **1:1 -- "fields of Moab"** (שְׂדֵי מוֹאָב): The plural construct "fields of" is used throughout Ruth, not the singular "field of." The Leningrad Codex is consistent in this form. In v. 6, the singular שְׂדֵה מוֹאָב appears for the second occurrence, possibly distinguishing the territorial region (singular) from the agricultural lands (plural).

2. **1:8 -- hesed** (חֶסֶד): This key theological term is rendered "lovingkindness" in the literal translation. It encompasses covenantal loyalty, steadfast love, and faithful kindness -- far exceeding any single English word. Naomi attributes hesed to her daughters-in-law for their faithfulness to the dead and to her.

3. **1:13 -- "more than you"** (מִכֶּם): This phrase is ambiguous. It could mean "more bitter than [it is for] you" or "more bitter on your account." The literal rendering preserves this ambiguity.

4. **1:14 -- davaq** (דָּבַק): Ruth "clung" to Naomi. This is the same verb used in Gen 2:24 of a man clinging to his wife. Its use here signals the covenantal depth of Ruth's commitment.

5. **1:20 -- Naomi/Mara**: Naomi (נָעֳמִי) means "my-pleasantness" or "my-delight." Mara (מָרָא) means "bitter." The wordplay is central to the narrative theology of the chapter.

6. **1:21 -- "testified against me"** (עָנָה בִי): The verb עָנָה can mean "answer" or "testify against." The Leningrad Codex vocalization supports the judicial sense of divine testimony.

-- EMET
